params.markdown:
# Indices and datastreams usage dashboard

This dashboard will require the `logs-elasticsearch.index_pivot-default` [transform](/app/management/data/transform) to be activated.

This dashboard will provide tier- and datastream-centric consumption information, such as:
* Ingest volume (written on disk, after indexing by Elasticsearch).
* Query volume (by count and time).
* Storage volume.


This will help you review your data usage and ensure alignment between expectations, usage patterns, ILM policy and architecture best practices.

embeddableConfig.description: Computed over the last 7 days of the viewed time range
Computed over the last 7 days of the viewed time range

This displays the querying time, function of how old the queried indices are. Querying volume usually decreases as the index ages. Best practices would recommend to align retention with querying profile, and storing data in colder tier as its consumption volume decreases.

This displays the primary stored volume, function of how old the indices are. As the indices age, they should typically be stored in colder tiers, unless there's a business justification for doing otherwise.
Primary store excludes considerations of replications or partial-mounting of snapshots.

The active store considers both data replication and searchable snapshot usage (where applicable).

For an index with 1 primary and 1 replica shard, the total active store would be double of the primary store.

For a fully-mounted index in the cold tier, the active store would be equal to the primary store (as the replica is a snapshot, stored outside of cluster nodes).

For a partially-mounted index in the frozen tier, the active store would be a fraction of the primary store, as the data structures are loaded on-demand upon user queries.
Primary store excludes considerations of replications or partial-mounting of snapshots.
